channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: PC Gaming’s Dominance is Reshaping The Industry
item.content:encoded:
In the last few years, especially post-COVID, we’ve seen a massive rise in the number of people gaming on PC platforms. It has become something of a paradigm shift, with more people adopting a PC-driven setup than a console-backed one, which has led to both positive and negative trends surfacing.

In a new 80-page report published by NewZoo, it was confirmed in no unclear terms that PC is fast becoming the most dominant force in the business. By 2028, it’ll be making more money than console platforms, it already has far more players, and it’s driving the emergence of new monetization models that are too lucrative for anyone to ignore.

If You’re Ignoring PC in 2026, You’re Doing it Wrong

NewZoo’s new report revealed an interesting nugget of information. Using the data available, it has been estimated that, by 2028, PC revenue will surpass console revenue in every way, with the latter relying on blockbuster releases to firm up its earnings potential.

This growth will be led by Asian expansion, a growing share of PC distribution amongst Gen Z and Gen Alpha gamers, higher average selling prices, and Valve’s ecosystem expanding to include more hardware.

It doesn’t end there.

By 2028, PC could surpass one billion players, while console platforms will lag behind with just 688 million, globally. Right now, the PC player base is floating around 966 million, which puts consoles to shame already, but it’ll explode over the next few years, given the expected trends.

It’s worth pointing out that both of them are dwarfed by the number of players enjoying mobile-based games, but that’s a topic for another day.

NewZoo also drew attention to an emerging trend that’s seeing $30 – $50 games soar at launch, noting that sub-$30 games are thriving in the PC space, especially. The dominance of breakout indie games constantly boosts PC’s performance in that respect.

It was noted that a painful trend still exists on consoles. The lion’s share of all money made goes to the top twenty games in the ecosystem, but in the PC space, more than half of all revenue is attributed to games beyond that ranking. We’re also seeing a ‘structural decline’ of AAA-driven genres, which is no big secret in 2026.

It’s also a damning fact that PC is supporting the industry on mighty shoulders. Between 2024 and 2025, playtime dropped on Xbox by 3% and on PlayStation by 4%, but PC saw a 3% increase as it soaks up the majority market share.

What’s holding the players in the PC space, then?

NewZoo revealed that Roblox, Counter-Strike 2, and League of Legends are in the top spots for PC players. They’re followed by Minecraft, Fortnite, Dota 2, and VALORANT. Beyond that, it’s World of Warcraft, The Sims 4, Call of Duty, and Escape from Tarkov.

That being said, the growth isn’t just coming from premium titles. In major markets, PC’s free-to-play revenue is soaring, almost doubling that of PlayStation and Xbox games. Games like League of Legends and Counter-Strike will always keep that figure high, though.

Are you really surprised that PC is dominating consoles, and will do so to a more extreme scale going forward? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Microsoft is deleting traces of This is an Xbox marketing campaign

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/pc-gaming-dominance-reshaping-industry/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Clair Obscur and Dispatch Lead BAFTA Games Awards Nominations
item.content:encoded:
The full sweep of nominations has been revealed for the 2026 BAFTA Games Awards, and Clari Obscur: Expedition 33 and Dispatch are sitting at the top of the table. In the numbers, a whopping 17 studios have been nominated for the first time, and the majority of the nominees in the performance categories could also make their BAFTA Games debut.

Also making up the bulk of the nominations are games like Ghost of Yotei, Death Stranding 2: On the Beach, and ARC Raiders.

All Nominations for the BAFTA Games Awards 2026

The BAFTA organisation has lifted the lid on the full list of nominations for this year’s BAFTA Games Awards, the 22nd event in the showcase’s history. In a statement, Tara Saunders, chair of BAFTA’s Games Committee, said:

Today’s nominations recognise 42 outstanding games that highlight the very best of the global games industry. This year’s nominations reflect an industry reshaping itself – with a vibrant mix of established studios standing alongside a wide range of independent studios, and a number of development teams earning nominations for the first time. The exceptional craft, artistry and innovation behind these titles shines through.

A nomination is an extraordinary achievement, and I extend my warmest congratulations to every nominee for their innovation, hard work and resilience.

That being said, here’s the full array of nominations for this year’s awards show.

Animation

  • Battlefield 6
  • Death Stranding 2
  • Dispatch PREDICTION
  • Ghost of Yotei
  • Hades II
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong

Artistic Achievement

  • Clair Obscur
  • Death Stranding 2
  • Dispatch
  • Ghost of Yotei
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong
  • South of Midnight PREDICTION

Audio Achievement

  • ARC Raiders PREDICTION
  • Clair Obscur
  • Death Stranding 2
  • Dispatch
  • Ghost of Yotei
  • Indiana Jones

Best Game

  • Clair Obscur PREDICTION
  • Dispatch
  • Ghost of Yotei
  • Indiana Jones
  • ARC Raiders
  • Blue Prince

British Game

  • Atomfall
  • Citizen Sleeper 2
  • Mafia: The Old Country PREDICTION
  • Monument Valley 3
  • Powerwash Simulator 2
  • Two Point Museum

Debut Game

  • Blue Prince
  • Clair Obscur
  • Consume Me
  • Despelote
  • Dispatch PREDICTION
  • The Midnight Walk

Evolving Game

  • Vampire Survivors
  • Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2
  • Fallout 76
  • Helldivers 2
  • Hitman: World of Assassination
  • No Man’s Sky PREDICTION

Family

  • Donkey Kong Bananza PREDICTION
  • Is This Seat Taken?
  • LEGO Party!
  • Mario Kart World
  • Powerwash Simulator 2
  • Two Point Museum

Game Beyond Entertainment

  • The Alters PREDICTION
  • And Roger
  • Citizen Sleeper 2
  • Consume Me
  • Despelote
  • STALKER 2

Game Design

  • Ball X Pit
  • Blue Prince PREDICTION
  • Clair Obscur
  • Ghost of Yotei
  • Hades II
  • Split Fiction

Multiplayer

  • ARC Raiders PREDICTION
  • Dune: Awakening
  • Elden Ring Nightreign
  • LEGO Party!
  • PEAK
  • Split Fiction

Music

  • Clair Obsur
  • Death Stranding 2 PREDICTION
  • Dispatch
  • Ghost of Yotei
  • Hollow Knight: Silksong
  • Indiana Jones

Narrative

  • The Alters
  • Blue Prince
  • Clair Obscur
  • Death Stranding 2
  • Indiana Jones
  • Kingdom Come Deliverance II PREDICTION

New IP

  • The Alters
  • ARC Raiders PREDICTION
  • Clair Obscur
  • Dispatch
  • South of Midnight
  • Split Fiction

Performer in a Leading Role

  • Aaron Paul (Dispatch)
  • Ben Starr (Clair Obscur)
  • Erika Ishii (Ghost of Yotei)
  • Jennifer English (Clair Obscur)
  • Tom McKay (Kingdom Come: Deliverance II) PREDICTION
  • Troy Baker (Indiana Jones)

Performer in a Supporting Role

  • Alix Wilton Regan (Lies of P)
  • Charlie Cox (Clair Obscur)
  • Jane Perry (Dead Take) PREDICTION
  • Jeffrey Wright (Dispatch)
  • Kirsty Rider (Clair Obscur)
  • Troy Baker (Death Stranding 2)

Technical Achievement

  • Indiana Jones
  • Split Fiction
  • ARC Raiders PREDICTION
  • Death Stranding 2
  • DOOM: The Dark Ages
  • Ghost of Yotei

The 22nd BAFTA Games Awards will air on Friday, April 17.

Let us know what you think about the list of nominations and if your predictions align with ours on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Escape from Tarkov went big in 2025

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: League of Legends’ LPL Attracts Highest Viewership In Years
item.content:encoded:
League of Legends fans are interested in the LPL again as the league attracted its highest viewership outside of China in the last couple of years.

Excluding the hard-to-verify Chinese viewership, the LPL 2026 Split 1 final drew in a peak audience of over 251k viewers, making it the most-watched LPL event since Spring 2024’s peak of over 292k according to Esports Charts.

Two of China’s biggest teams, Bilibili Gaming (BLG) and JD Gaming (JDG), clashed in a five-game thriller during the LPL 2026 Split 1 final. The fact that it was two prestigious orgs and a full five-game series heavily benefited the viewing figures. Plus, the two behemoths had not faced each other in a final since Spring 2023.

Meanwhile, the LPL’s peak viewership came in 2020 at just shy of 350k when Top Esports and JDG clashed in the Summer Playoff finals. This was during the league’s heyday, coming off the back of LPL teams winning the previous two LoL Worlds tournaments (Invictus Gaming in 2018 and FunPlus Phoenix (FPX) in 2019. Before that, an LPL team had never lifted the Summoner’s Cup.

Only one other LPL team has won Worlds, and that was EDward Gaming in 2021. Though Weibo Gaming and BLG finished as runners-up in 2023 and 2024, respectively.

LPL struggles

There have been concerns over the future of the LPL for a while now, with petering interest and financial issues.

In 2025, Riot decided to stop running an official English broadcast for the LPL itself. Instead, it opened up the co-streaming rights and a lot of up-and-coming broadcast talent banded together to create their own broadcast while mastering their craft.

Additionally, this year’s LPL features two fewer teams after the departures of two of its most high-profile organisations, former World Champions FPX and one of China’s most decorated teams in Royal Never Give Up.

Despite this, the league is still going strong and big-name players like Zhuo ‘Knight’ Ding and Chen ‘GALA’ Wei continue to draw viewers in.

Plus, the league has been boosted by the co-streams of Brazilian creator Gustavo ‘baiano’ Gomes, whose LPL streams overtook that of Marc ‘Caedrel’ Lamont in terms of hours watched.

Still, it will always be difficult for the LPL to compete with the dedicated Western audience that South Korea’s LCK has. Especially with T1 and Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok.

Do you enjoy watching the LPL or just wait for international tournaments? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And find out who LPL finalists BLG and JDG will face in the LoL First Stand opening matches.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Escape from Tarkov Was The Most Popular ‘New PC Release’ in 2025
item.content:encoded:
In a new access-all-areas report published by NewZoo, we’ve been given a look at the state of play for the entire gaming ecosystem through 2025 and beyond. In one segment, playtime for new titles in 2025 was discussed at length, uncovering an extremely interesting figure.

Escape from Tarkov was the most played new PC release in 2025, with a caveat that it was released from early access, giving it a strong edge in the numbers. Even so, it managed to beat the likes of Battlefield 6 and ARC Raiders, both of which sold millions of copies and skyrocketed up the charts.

It’ll be interesting to see how the numbers look now that ARC Raiders has dominated the extraction market for months, but NewZoo’s report is clear in the figures. Escape from Tarkov, from Battlestate Games, was the most played new PC release in 2025, capturing 1.7% of the market share.

By contrast, Battlefield 6 and REDSEC assumed 0.8%, and ARC Raiders picked up 0.6%. That’s even with both launching weeks before Escape from Tarkov hit 1.0 in November.

It gets even more impressive.

According to NewZoo’s data, Escape from Tarkov was the third-most-played new release across all platforms in 2025, securing 0.8% of playtime even with the slew of games that dropped last year muscling in on the numbers.

Now, consider that Escape from Tarkov is available exclusively on PC, and it becomes even more surprising. There’s a console release in the works, but it could be years before it surfaces.

On console platforms specifically, the likes of Monster Hunter Wilds, Battlefield 6, ARC Raiders, Elden Ring Nightreign, Helldivers 2, and Oblivion Remastered all did quite well for themselves.

Again, it’s important to stress that, while technically a ‘new release’, Escape from Tarkov did come from early access.

In the vast sea of extraction titles, Escape from Tarkov still stands atop the mountain, even amid some issues surfacing amongst the community and more competition than ever rising to threaten the game.

Are you surprised that Escape from Tarkov secured these numbers in 2025? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Marathon already has an esports tournament lined up

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Battle Royale Games Collapse as Sandbox Titles Soar Amid New Trends
item.content:encoded:
It’s finally being confirmed in the data. The world is moving on from battle royale titles and is instead focusing on sandbox games, adopting that sense of freedom and choice over being the last man standing. In a new report from NewZoo, it was confirmed that battle royale games saw a monumental drop of -27% in playtime in 2025 versus 2024.

By contrast, sandbox titles grew a whopping 36% in playtime in the same window. There were also some minor declines in the shooter and role-playing space, while adventure games also saw minor growth.

Survival in a Different Way

It seems to be the new trend these days. We’re seeing survival-sandbox games soar in almost every respect, as players settle in and build a life for themselves in some digital universe, often opposed by a traditional threat: zombies, raiders, the elements, and so on. That’s how most sandbox games work.

Battle royale titles, which saw a boom from 2019 onwards with the likes of Fortnite, Apex Legends, and Call of Duty: Warzone skyrocketing, had a monumental drop across all platforms in 2025. The decline isn’t much of a surprise, as it’s effectively common knowledge that the battle royale experience is dying out.

Even Fortnite is leaning into alternate entertainment to keep fans engaged, standing up the UEFN ecosystem and offering all kinds of games and side modes away from the battle royale core.

It gets even more impressive when you realise that sandbox games are flying up over every other genre despite some major releases in the last year. For instance, Battlefield 6, the best-selling game of last year, a first-person shooter, and ARC Raiders, a transformative extraction shooter that racked up millions of sales, both dropped in 2025.

It’s not a good look for these top-tier battle royale games, which are failing to secure traction going forward. Apex Legends had a 24% drop in playtime in 2025, and Fortnite recorded a 29% drop. Shooters also suffered immensely:

  • Call of Duty: -33%
  • VALORANT: -15%
  • Rainbow Six Siege: -24%
  • Overwatch: -29%
  • Destiny 2: -40%

Even Counter-Strike and Marvel Rivals couldn’t save shooters, and that’s with Marvel Rivals seeing a 546% increase in players in 2025. Although that figure is skewed, given that Marvel Rivals dropped in December 2024.

The numbers for 2026 will be much more interesting to view when they’re released, given that ARC Raiders has blown up and will understandably bolster results.

Are you surprised to learn that battle royale games are disappearing? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the recently proposed Tarkov changes

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Can You Preload Crimson Desert?
item.content:encoded:
The excitement is really mounting surrounding Crimson Desert, as the upcoming open-world behemoth is almost ready to tackle. It’s the kind of release that fans will call in sick for school or work for, but part of maximising the play time is making sure it’s ready for launch.

Some developers allow you to preload the game before launch, so as soon as the clock strikes release time, the game is ready for you to play from minute one. Many out there will be hoping that Pearl Abyss will offer the same treatment for Crimson Desert, and if you’re one of them, there’s some good news for you down below.

Can You Preload Crimson Desert Before Launch?

Yes, you can preload Crimson Desert before launch, meaning you’ll be able to have the game downloaded and ready for its March 19 launch day.

Crimson Desert is having a simultaneous worldwide release, meaning it’ll go live across all devices everywhere in the world at the same time, regardless of time zones. Therefore, you could be playing it at the crack of dawn or the middle of the day, but it’s certain that it’ll be at the same time as everybody else.

  • More Insider Gaming: Crimson Desert Release Date Countdown

That means that some fans won’t be able to spoil the opening hours for those who might have to wait. Instead, everybody can dive into the world of Pywel together, and here are the exact times you can prepare for that journey.

When Can You Preload Crimson Desert?

You can preload Crimson Desert two days before its official launch, meaning it’ll be available for download on March 17.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Crimson Desert Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices

As mentioned before, the game will have a worldwide release, so that date might be different depending on where you live. Thankfully, Insider Gaming has broken down all of the preload times across a whole variety of time zones, so you can plan your adventures accordingly:

Are you excited to finally play Crimson Desert? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Crimson Desert content, check out the Crimson Desert launch trailer, as well as whether it’s a sequel or prequel to Black Desert Online.

MOBILE APP
